headline: IAC to Participate in the 2026 Annual J.P. Morgan Global Technology, Media and Communications Conference
summary: IAC (NASDAQ: IAC) will participate in the Annual J.P. Morgan Global Technology, Media and Communications Conference on Tuesday, May 19, 2026. Christopher Halpin, Executive Vice President, COO and CFO of IAC and Tim Quinn, CFO of People Inc. will participate in a fireside chat at 2:55 p.m. ET. Both a live audio webcast and replay of the presentation will be available to the public in the IR section of IAC's website at https://ir.iac.com/events-and-presentations.

headline: Is It Too Late To Consider JPMorgan Chase (JPM) After Its Strong Multi‑Year Rally?
summary: If you are wondering whether JPMorgan Chase at around US$300 a share still offers value or is looking stretched, the key is to line up what you are paying against what you are actually getting. The stock has pulled back recently, with the price down about 2.5% over the past week and 3.2% over the past month, while still showing a 17.6% gain over the last year and a very large 3 year return of 137.5% and 5 year return of 106.7%. headline: Bank Stock Buybacks Hit a Record in First Quarter. Citi, BofA, and Goldman Were Leaders.
summary: The country’s largest banks, flush with record earnings and capital, executed their largest quarterly stock repurchases ever in the first three months of the year.  The 21 large banks covered by Barclays analyst Jason Goldberg bought back $40 billion in the first quarter, up from $34 billion in the fourth quarter of 2025 and from the prior record of $38 billion in the 2019 fourth quarter, just before the Covid crisis.  The industry leaders, based on the biggest percentage reductions in share counts in the first quarter, were  M&T Bank  (3.9% reduction),  First Citizens Bancshares  (3.8%),  Citi  group (3.1%),  Bank of America (1.9%), and  Goldman Sachs Group  (1.8%).

headline: How The Schneider Electric (ENXTPA:SU) Investment Story Is Shifting With Conflicting Analyst Targets
summary: Schneider Electric’s updated fair value of €300.55, compared with the earlier €296.50, comes against a backdrop of mixed price targets that run from about €290 up to €340. Bullish analysts lifting targets toward €325 to €340 see scope above the new fair value, while more cautious calls around €292 flag concerns that much of the upside may already be in the stock. As you read on, you will see how these differing targets shape the evolving analyst narrative and what that might mean for how you...
